39:5517.name: The Engagement Play-book (and Why We Set It Aside)
39:5517.characters: The Engagement Play-book (and Why We Set It Aside)

1:1712.name: The yPrime platform launched with 250+ languages and full FDA and EMA regulatory approval. Greater interface clarity led to a measurable decrease in support calls from confused participants. Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform. We built a system that bridges different generations and geographies, and gives researchers a tool they can trust to collect clean data for years to come.
1:1712.characters: The yPrime platform launched with 250+ languages and full FDA and EMA regulatory approval. Greater interface clarity led to a measurable decrease in support calls from confused participants. Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform. We built a system that bridges different generations and geographies, and gives researchers a tool they can trust to collect clean data for years to come.

1:1705.name: Participants can review study documents section by section at their own pace, highlight passages, annotate questions, and flag anything before signing. The system supports multi-party consent for minors, reconsent when protocols change mid-trial, and medical consultation during review. Every feature was built around one principle: no signature before genuine understanding.
1:1705.characters: Participants can review study documents section by section at their own pace, highlight passages, annotate questions, and flag anything before signing. The system supports multi-party consent for minors, reconsent when protocols change mid-trial, and medical consultation during review. Every feature was built around one principle: no signature before genuine understanding.

1:1696.name: The problem is not that these mechanics fail to drive behaviour. It is that the behaviour they drive is the wrong kind. A participant chasing a streak is no longer reporting how they genuinely feel. They are performing compliance. In clinical research, clean data is worth more than high engagement. We built the argument in research, presented it with data, and held the line.
1:1696.characters: The problem is not that these mechanics fail to drive behaviour. It is that the behaviour they drive is the wrong kind. A participant chasing a streak is no longer reporting how they genuinely feel. They are performing compliance. In clinical research, clean data is worth more than high engagement. We built the argument in research, presented it with data, and held the line.

1:1691.name: Stakeholders raised gamification early. It was a reasonable instinct. We took it seriously. We tested progress rings, completion streaks, and compliance scores, etc. across participant cohorts. Then we set every one of them aside.
1:1691.characters: Stakeholders raised gamification early. It was a reasonable instinct. We took it seriously. We tested progress rings, completion streaks, and compliance scores, etc. across participant cohorts. Then we set every one of them aside.

1:1690.name: The Engagement Play-book (and Why We Set It Aside)
1:1690.characters: The Engagement Play-book (and Why We Set It Aside)

1:1675.name: Every ECOA user is unique by age, language, country, and device. But so is every clinical study. The challenge was building a system flexible enough for research variation while remaining emotionally consistent for participants. Everything was broken into modules with clear groupings and variations. Progress indicators, text alignment, copyright attribution per screen, branching logic, input types: each one configurable by the research team, invisible in its complexity to the participant.
1:1675.characters: Every ECOA user is unique by age, language, country, and device. But so is every clinical study. The challenge was building a system flexible enough for research variation while remaining emotionally consistent for participants. Everything was broken into modules with clear groupings and variations. Progress indicators, text alignment, copyright attribution per screen, branching logic, input types: each one configurable by the research team, invisible in its complexity to the participant.

1:1683.name: Interaction had to feel grounded and intuitive. We avoided flashy transitions that could confuse or impress, instead designing motion that mirrors real-world metaphors. Screens slide like paper across a desk. Surveys drop down like a document handed to you and lift away when complete. Pages of equal hierarchy slide horizontally, aligned with their place in navigation. These transitions form a consistent mental map: users always know where they are and what comes next.
1:1683.characters: Interaction had to feel grounded and intuitive. We avoided flashy transitions that could confuse or impress, instead designing motion that mirrors real-world metaphors. Screens slide like paper across a desk. Surveys drop down like a document handed to you and lift away when complete. Pages of equal hierarchy slide horizontally, aligned with their place in navigation. These transitions form a consistent mental map: users always know where they are and what comes next.

1:1666.name: We tested over 100 combinations against emotional keyword associations and AAA contrast standards. We landed on a calm, mid-spectrum purple — chosen not because it looked good in a presentation, but because it evoked nothing in particular. In this context, that restraint is exactly the point.
1:1666.characters: We tested over 100 combinations against emotional keyword associations and AAA contrast standards. We landed on a calm, mid-spectrum purple — chosen not because it looked good in a presentation, but because it evoked nothing in particular. In this context, that restraint is exactly the point.

39:5507.name: One of the earliest challenges was adaptability, not just technical performance. The app needed to work across screen sizes, system settings, and languages in a real way: accommodating long labels, different reading directions, and regional interface norms. Internationalisation touched every level of the design system, from type hierarchies that support complex character sets to layout logic that respects bidirectional text. The result is a product that adapts not only technically, but culturally.
39:5507.characters: One of the earliest challenges was adaptability, not just technical performance. The app needed to work across screen sizes, system settings, and languages in a real way: accommodating long labels, different reading directions, and regional interface norms. Internationalisation touched every level of the design system, from type hierarchies that support complex character sets to layout logic that respects bidirectional text. The result is a product that adapts not only technically, but culturally.

1:1654.name: Behind every decision, every animation, layout pattern, piece of copy there was a research. Qualitative interviews, usability testing, quantitative surveys, and exploratory studies across geographies and ability profiles. Bias is invisible. In clinical trials, even small friction can mean lost data or broken trust. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them.
1:1654.characters: Behind every decision, every animation, layout pattern, piece of copy there was a research. Qualitative interviews, usability testing, quantitative surveys, and exploratory studies across geographies and ability profiles. Bias is invisible. In clinical trials, even small friction can mean lost data or broken trust. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them.

1:1647.name: The tools participants used every day were never designed for them. And nobody could tell, because in this industry, nobody talks. I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on one foundational idea: emotional neutrality is not a design preference in clinical research. It is a clinical requirement. The platform had to serve users aged eight to ninety, across 250+ languages, with zero behavioural nudging.
1:1647.characters: The tools participants used every day were never designed for them. And nobody could tell, because in this industry, nobody talks. I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on one foundational idea: emotional neutrality is not a design preference in clinical research. It is a clinical requirement. The platform had to serve users aged eight to ninety, across 250+ languages, with zero behavioural nudging.

1:1639.name: With no competitive landscape to draw from, research became the only compass. We ran qualitative interviews, usability sessions, and quantitative studies across multiple geographies and participant profiles. From tech-hesitant seniors to participants with visual impairments to people whose first language used a non-Latin script. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them. The most important early finding was that simplicity and predictability are not just usability goals here they are clinical tools. The faster a daily routine forms around the app, the lower the dropout risk. This reframed the entire problem. We were not building something people would enjoy. We were building an instrument people could rely on. Colour was chosen with the same rigour. A calm, mid-spectrum purple was selected not because it tested well aesthetically, but because it tested as nothing. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies approval. Purple at the right saturation evokes very little and in a context where emotional neutrality is a requirement, that absence of meaning is precisely the point. Shape carried equal weight: every core function was defined by predictable placement and distinct geometry, not colour alone. Blur all the text and if the interface still communicates its purpose, it is working.
1:1639.characters:
With no competitive landscape to draw from, research became the only compass. We ran qualitative interviews, usability sessions, and quantitative studies across multiple geographies and participant profiles. From tech-hesitant seniors to participants with visual impairments to people whose first language used a non-Latin script. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them.

The most important early finding was that simplicity and predictability are not just usability goals here they are clinical tools. The faster a daily routine forms around the app, the lower the dropout risk. This reframed the entire problem. We were not building something people would enjoy. We were building an instrument people could rely on.

Colour was chosen with the same rigour. A calm, mid-spectrum purple was selected not because it tested well aesthetically, but because it tested as nothing. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies approval. Purple at the right saturation evokes very little and in a context where emotional neutrality is a requirement, that absence of meaning is precisely the point. Shape carried equal weight: every core function was defined by predictable placement and distinct geometry, not colour alone. Blur all the text and if the interface still communicates its purpose, it is working.

1:1718.name: Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform.

1:1636.name: Completion rates rose, trial abandonment dropped, and study setup time compressed, while participants from rural Poland to urban Nigeria felt the platform was built for them.

1:1630.name: I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on emotional neutrality as a clinical requirement and has to serve users aged 14 to 90 across 250+ languages with zero behavioural nudging.

1:1624.name: 30% of clinical trial participants drop out before completion. Re-recruiting a single patient costs $19,000. The tools they used daily were never designed for them.

1:1671.name: 💡 A useful test: blur all the text and remove colour. If the interface still communicates its purpose, it is working.

1:1591.name: YPrime: Designing for everyone — Foundational design principles for a clinical research tool.
1:1591.characters: YPrime: Designing for everyone — Foundational design principles for a clinical research tool.

1:1493.name: Participants moved through flows faster and with fewer errors.
1:1493.characters: Participants moved through flows faster and with fewer errors.

1:1400.name: Measurable reductions in time-on-task and error rates validated adoption, while accelerating​ time to insight, supporting smarter decisions, and ultimately driving stronger commercial outcomes, increased savings delivery, and resilience.
1:1400.characters: Measurable reductions in time-on-task and error rates validated adoption, while accelerating​ time to insight, supporting smarter decisions, and ultimately driving stronger commercial outcomes, increased savings delivery, and resilience.

1:1616.name: Completion rates rose, trial abandonment dropped, and study setup time compressed, while participants from rural Poland to urban Nigeria felt the platform was built for them.
1:1616.characters: Completion rates rose, trial abandonment dropped, and study setup time compressed, while participants from rural Poland to urban Nigeria felt the platform was built for them.

39:5600.name: In most digital products, engagement is everything. In clinical research, engagement must not distort behaviour. We tested common engagement techniques — progress rings, streaks, compliance scores — and rejected them all. They introduced emotional pressure, reward-seeking, or bias that could compromise data integrity. Instead, we practiced invisible encouragement. After completing a task or training, users receive a small, passive badge — no sound, no distracting animation, no complex interaction. Just a quiet signal of closure: thank you, you’re done!
39:5600.characters: In most digital products, engagement is everything. In clinical research, engagement must not distort behaviour. 1:1657.name: Understanding the backbone of the project came before any screen work. How does the flow look for the patient, the doctor, the study coordinator, the caregiver? What data passes to the server? What errors can occur from connection loss? Which APIs are in play, where do they go, and does the user need feedback while that happens? Every actor in the system was mapped through their own journey before a single shared component was drawn.

39:5515.name: The homepage is the clearest expression of the philosophy. It shows exactly what is due today. If nothing is scheduled, the space stays empty — deliberately. In an attention economy built on filling every moment, that silence is a design decision that takes real conviction to defend.

39:5451.name: Consultants relied on a fragmented ecosystem of disconnected tools, forcing manual data bridging and slowing decisions across complex, multi-client engagements.

39:5553.name: System that bridges the gap between different generations, accessibility needs and geographies.
39:5553.characters: System that bridges the gap between different generations, accessibility needs and geographies.

39:5457.name: I led end-to-end UX across multiple workstreams within an Agile delivery environment, establishing a continuous discovery loop using Lean UX principles.
39:5457.characters: I led end-to-end UX across multiple workstreams within an Agile delivery environment, establishing a continuous discovery loop using Lean UX principles.

39:5489.name: Colour in clinical UX carries more weight than in almost any other context. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies clearance. We needed something that arrived without baggage, a colour participants would encounter daily for months without triggering an association that could quietly skew how they reported their symptoms.

39:5526.name: What we kept was the quietest possible form of positive reinforcement: a small, non-interactive badge after task or training completion. No sound. No animation. No demand for acknowledgement. Just a brief signal of closure. Even this was tested extensively across cultural contexts, not for delight, but for emotional safety.

After completing a task or training, users receive a small, passive badge — no sound, no distracting animation, no complex interaction. Just a quiet signal of closure: thank you, you’re done!

39:5532.name: Participants can review study documents section by section at their own pace, highlight passages, annotate questions, and flag anything before signing. The system supports multi-party consent for minors, reconsent when protocols change mid-trial, and medical consultation during review. Every feature was built around one principle: no signature before genuine understanding.
39:5532.characters: Participants can review study documents section by section at their own pace, highlight passages, annotate questions, and flag anything before signing. The system supports multi-party consent for minors, reconsent when protocols change mid-trial, and medical consultation during review. Every feature was built around one principle: no signature before genuine understanding.

1:1672.name: Shape carried equal weight. Every core function — confirming, navigating, submitting — was defined by predictable placement, distinct geometry, and clear affordance, with colour as a reinforcing layer rather than the primary signal.
1:1672.characters: Shape carried equal weight. Every core function — confirming, navigating, submitting — was defined by predictable placement, distinct geometry, and clear affordance, with colour as a reinforcing layer rather than the primary signal.

39:5437.name: I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on emotional neutrality as a clinical requirement and has to serve users aged 14 to 90 across 250+ languages with zero behavioural nudging.
39:5437.characters: I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on emotional neutrality as a clinical requirement and has to serve users aged 14 to 90 across 250+ languages with zero behavioural nudging.

1:1403.name: With no competitive landscape to draw from, research became the only compass. We ran qualitative interviews, usability sessions, and quantitative studies across multiple geographies and participant profiles. From tech-hesitant seniors to participants with visual impairments to people whose first language used a non-Latin script. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them. The most important early finding was that simplicity and predictability are not just usability goals here they are clinical tools. The faster a daily routine forms around the app, the lower the dropout risk. This reframed the entire problem. We were not building something people would enjoy. We were building an instrument people could rely on. Colour was chosen with the same rigour. A calm, mid-spectrum purple was selected not because it tested well aesthetically, but because it tested as nothing. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies approval. Purple at the right saturation evokes very little and in a context where emotional neutrality is a requirement, that absence of meaning is precisely the point. Shape carried equal weight: every core function was defined by predictable placement and distinct geometry, not colour alone. Blur all the text and if the interface still communicates its purpose, it is working.
1:1403.characters:
With no competitive landscape to draw from, research became the only compass. We ran qualitative interviews, usability sessions, and quantitative studies across multiple geographies and participant profiles. From tech-hesitant seniors to participants with visual impairments to people whose first language used a non-Latin script. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them.

The most important early finding was that simplicity and predictability are not just usability goals here they are clinical tools. The faster a daily routine forms around the app, the lower the dropout risk. This reframed the entire problem. We were not building something people would enjoy. We were building an instrument people could rely on.

Colour was chosen with the same rigour. A calm, mid-spectrum purple was selected not because it tested well aesthetically, but because it tested as nothing. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies approval. Purple at the right saturation evokes very little and in a context where emotional neutrality is a requirement, that absence of meaning is precisely the point. Shape carried equal weight: every core function was defined by predictable placement and distinct geometry, not colour alone. Blur all the text and if the interface still communicates its purpose, it is working.

39:5523.name: The problem is not that these mechanics fail to drive behaviour. It is that the behaviour they drive is the wrong kind. A participant chasing a streak is no longer reporting how they genuinely feel. They are performing compliance. In clinical research, clean data is worth more than high engagement. We built the argument in research, presented it with data, and held the line.
39:5523.characters: The problem is not that these mechanics fail to drive behaviour. It is that the behaviour they drive is the wrong kind. A participant chasing a streak is no longer reporting how they genuinely feel. They are performing compliance. In clinical research, clean data is worth more than high engagement. We built the argument in research, presented it with data, and held the line.

39:5481.name: Behind every decision, every animation, layout pattern, piece of copy there was a research. Qualitative interviews, usability testing, quantitative surveys, and exploratory studies across geographies and ability profiles. Bias is invisible. In clinical trials, even small friction can mean lost data or broken trust. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them.
39:5481.characters: Behind every decision, every animation, layout pattern, piece of copy there was a research. Qualitative interviews, usability testing, quantitative surveys, and exploratory studies across geographies and ability profiles. Bias is invisible. In clinical trials, even small friction can mean lost data or broken trust. We did not validate assumptions. We questioned them.

1:1411.name: The tools participants used every day were never designed for them. And nobody could tell, because in this industry, nobody talks. I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on one foundational idea: emotional neutrality is not a design preference in clinical research. It is a clinical requirement. The platform had to serve users aged eight to ninety, across 250+ languages, with zero behavioural nudging.
1:1411.characters: The tools participants used every day were never designed for them. And nobody could tell, because in this industry, nobody talks. I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on one foundational idea: emotional neutrality is not a design preference in clinical research. It is a clinical requirement. The platform had to serve users aged eight to ninety, across 250+ languages, with zero behavioural nudging.

1:1473.name: Alongside the core platform, I designed eConsent from the ground up, taking it from an initial concept to a fully architected, validated, developer-ready product. This was not a digital signature tool. It was a framework for genuine comprehension.
1:1473.characters: Alongside the core platform, I designed eConsent from the ground up, taking it from an initial concept to a fully architected, validated, developer-ready product. This was not a digital signature tool. It was a framework for genuine comprehension.

1:1461.name: The Engagement Play-book (and Why We Set It Aside)
1:1461.characters: The Engagement Play-book (and Why We Set It Aside)

1:1394.name: I led end-to-end UX across multiple workstreams within an Agile delivery environment, establishing a continuous discovery loop using Lean UX principles.
1:1394.characters: I led end-to-end UX across multiple workstreams within an Agile delivery environment, establishing a continuous discovery loop using Lean UX principles.

39:5549.name: Participants moved through flows faster and with fewer errors.
39:5549.characters: Participants moved through flows faster and with fewer errors.

1:1434.name: We tested over 100 combinations against emotional keyword associations and AAA contrast standards. We landed on a calm, mid-spectrum purple — chosen not because it looked good in a presentation, but because it evoked nothing in particular. In this context, that restraint is exactly the point.
1:1434.characters: We tested over 100 combinations against emotional keyword associations and AAA contrast standards. We landed on a calm, mid-spectrum purple — chosen not because it looked good in a presentation, but because it evoked nothing in particular. In this context, that restraint is exactly the point.

1:1462.name: Stakeholders raised gamification early. It was a reasonable instinct. We took it seriously. We tested progress rings, completion streaks, and compliance scores, etc. across participant cohorts. Then we set every one of them aside.
1:1462.characters: Stakeholders raised gamification early. It was a reasonable instinct. We took it seriously. We tested progress rings, completion streaks, and compliance scores, etc. across participant cohorts. Then we set every one of them aside.

1:1459.name: The homepage is the clearest expression of the philosophy. It shows exactly what is due today. If nothing is scheduled, the space stays empty — deliberately. In an attention economy built on filling every moment, that silence is a design decision that takes real conviction to defend.
1:1459.characters: The homepage is the clearest expression of the philosophy. It shows exactly what is due today. If nothing is scheduled, the space stays empty — deliberately. In an attention economy built on filling every moment, that silence is a design decision that takes real conviction to defend.

1:1483.name: The yPrime platform launched with 250+ languages and full FDA and EMA regulatory approval. Greater interface clarity led to a measurable decrease in support calls from confused participants. Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform. We built a system that bridges different generations and geographies, and gives researchers a tool they can trust to collect clean data for years to come.
1:1483.characters: The yPrime platform launched with 250+ languages and full FDA and EMA regulatory approval. Greater interface clarity led to a measurable decrease in support calls from confused participants. Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform. We built a system that bridges different generations and geographies, and gives researchers a tool they can trust to collect clean data for years to come.

1:1688.name: The homepage is the clearest expression of the philosophy. It shows exactly what is due today. If nothing is scheduled, the space stays empty — deliberately. In an attention economy built on filling every moment, that silence is a design decision that takes real conviction to defend.

1:1497.name: System that bridges the gap between different generations, accessibility needs and geographies.
1:1497.characters: System that bridges the gap between different generations, accessibility needs and geographies.

1:1489.name: Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform.
1:1489.characters: Daily task completion rates climbed while missed submissions fell compared to the previous legacy platform.

1:1446.name: Every ECOA user is unique by age, language, country, and device. But so is every clinical study. The challenge was building a system flexible enough for research variation while remaining emotionally consistent for participants. Everything was broken into modules with clear groupings and variations. Progress indicators, text alignment, copyright attribution per screen, branching logic, input types: each one configurable by the research team, invisible in its complexity to the participant.
1:1446.characters: Every ECOA user is unique by age, language, country, and device. But so is every clinical study. The challenge was building a system flexible enough for research variation while remaining emotionally consistent for participants. Everything was broken into modules with clear groupings and variations. Progress indicators, text alignment, copyright attribution per screen, branching logic, input types: each one configurable by the research team, invisible in its complexity to the participant.

45:8748.name: I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on emotional neutrality as a clinical requirement and has to serve users aged 14 to 90 across 250+ languages with zero behavioural nudging.
45:8748.characters: I led the design of a research-driven eCOA platform built on emotional neutrality as a clinical requirement and has to serve users aged 14 to 90 across 250+ languages with zero behavioural nudging.

1:1426.name: Colour in clinical UX carries more weight than in almost any other context. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies clearance. We needed something that arrived without baggage, a colour participants would encounter daily for months without triggering an association that could quietly skew how they reported their symptoms.
1:1426.characters: Colour in clinical UX carries more weight than in almost any other context. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies clearance. We needed something that arrived without baggage, a colour participants would encounter daily for months without triggering an association that could quietly skew how they reported their symptoms.

1:1662.name: Colour in clinical UX carries more weight than in almost any other context. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies clearance. We needed something that arrived without baggage, a colour participants would encounter daily for months without triggering an association that could quietly skew how they reported their symptoms.
1:1662.characters: Colour in clinical UX carries more weight than in almost any other context. Red signals emergency. Sterile blue reads as cold and clinical. Green implies clearance. We needed something that arrived without baggage, a colour participants would encounter daily for months without triggering an association that could quietly skew how they reported their symptoms.

39:5463.name: Measurable reductions in time-on-task and error rates validated adoption, while accelerating​ time to insight, supporting smarter decisions, and ultimately driving stronger commercial outcomes, increased savings delivery, and resilience.
39:5463.characters: Measurable reductions in time-on-task and error rates validated adoption, while accelerating​ time to insight, supporting smarter decisions, and ultimately driving stronger commercial outcomes, increased savings delivery, and resilience.

39:5510.name: Interaction had to feel grounded and intuitive. We avoided flashy transitions that could confuse or impress, instead designing motion that mirrors real-world metaphors. Screens slide like paper across a desk. Surveys drop down like a document handed to you and lift away when complete. Pages of equal hierarchy slide horizontally, aligned with their place in navigation. These transitions form a consistent mental map: users always know where they are and what comes next.
39:5510.characters: Interaction had to feel grounded and intuitive. We avoided flashy transitions that could confuse or impress, instead designing motion that mirrors real-world metaphors. Screens slide like paper across a desk. Surveys drop down like a document handed to you and lift away when complete. Pages of equal hierarchy slide horizontally, aligned with their place in navigation. These transitions form a consistent mental map: users always know where they are and what comes next.

45:8734.name: Completion rates rose, trial abandonment dropped, and study setup time compressed, while participants from rural Poland to urban Nigeria felt the platform was built for them.

1:1388.name: Consultants relied on a fragmented ecosystem of disconnected tools, forcing manual data bridging and slowing decisions across complex, multi-client engagements.

39:5518.name: Stakeholders raised gamification early. It was a reasonable instinct. We took it seriously. We tested progress rings, completion streaks, and compliance scores, etc. across participant cohorts. Then we set every one of them aside.
39:5518.characters: Stakeholders raised gamification early. It was a reasonable instinct. We took it seriously. We tested progress rings, completion streaks, and compliance scores, etc. across participant cohorts. Then we set every one of them aside.

1:1421.name: Understanding the backbone of the project came before any screen work. How does the flow look for the patient, the doctor, the study coordinator, the caregiver? What data passes to the server? What errors can occur from connection loss? Which APIs are in play, where do they go, and does the user need feedback while that happens? Every actor in the system was mapped through their own journey before a single shared component was drawn.
1:1421.characters: Understanding the backbone of the project came before any screen work. How does the flow look for the patient, the doctor, the study coordinator, the caregiver? What data passes to the server? What errors can occur from connection loss? Which APIs are in play, where do they go, and does the user need feedback while that happens? Every actor in the system was mapped through their own journey before a single shared component was drawn.

1:1772.name: The Engagement Playbook (and Why We Set It Aside)
1:1772.characters: The Engagement Playbook (and Why We Set It Aside)

1:1443.name: Shape carried equal weight. Every core function — confirming, navigating, submitting — was defined by predictable placement, distinct geometry, and clear affordance, with colour as a reinforcing layer rather than the primary signal.
1:1443.characters: Shape carried equal weight. Every core function — confirming, navigating, submitting — was defined by predictable placement, distinct geometry, and clear affordance, with colour as a reinforcing layer rather than the primary signal.

1:1699.name: What we kept was the quietest possible form of positive reinforcement: a small, non-interactive badge after task or training completion. No sound. No animation. No demand for acknowledgement. Just a brief signal of closure. Even this was tested extensively across cultural contexts, not for delight, but for emotional safety.
1:1699.characters: What we kept was the quietest possible form of positive reinforcement: a small, non-interactive badge after task or training completion. No sound. No animation. No demand for acknowledgement. Just a brief signal of closure. Even this was tested extensively across cultural contexts, not for delight, but for emotional safety.

The challenge was building a system flexible enough for research variation while remaining emotionally consistent for participants. Everything was broken into modules with clear groupings and variations. Progress indicators, text alignment, copyright attribution per screen, branching logic, input types: each one configurable by the research team, invisible in its complexity to the participant.
